feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: ATC - How to Implement AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

     I have implemented ATC in my company and I need to configure AUTHORITY-CHECK. From what I have seen, there is no standard AUTHORITY-CHECK. Has anyone implemented ATC with AUTHORITY-CHECK? Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Impact of Changing Cost Center on Settlement Rule in IW32 After Order Release
entry.content: In transaction IW32, once the order has been released and a settlement rule has already been assigned, my question is: if I change the cost center in the “Location” – “Account Assignment” view, should this change be reflected in the settlement rule?

entry.title: Production Order Shows Production Versions without BOM assignment
entry.content:
From my knowledge, Production Versions without BOM assignment cannot be used for creating Production Orders (CO01)

My BOM Application is assigned as the standard "PP01" in Order Type Dependent Parameter Setting (OPL8)

However, when I create a production order, it allows selection of production version without BOM assignment.

I would like to ask if it is standard system behavior to be able to select production version with only routing assigned?

entry.title: hello! I am planning to have two different tax codes for triangulations.
entry.content: I would like to have two different tax code for simplified triangulations ex art. 141. One for purchase side and one for sale side. Do you know which transaction type should I use for purchase side? For sale I assume it should be MWS but I have some doubts for purchase. can you help?

entry.title: Output File Name changed in upper case automatically for program FAGL_CL_REGROUP
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We want to define Output File Name on program FAGL_CL_REGROUP, but the location path entered in lower case is automatically changed to upper case.

 

The problem is that upper case location is not readable on linux OS.

 

So instead having that : /apps/K_NICE/userdata/testspool

 

we have this location: /APPS/K_NICE/USERDATA/TESTSPOOL

entry.title: Issue Creating Custom Transactions ZIQ01 and ZIQ02 for Standard Transactions IQ01 and IQ02
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am attempting to create two custom transactions (ZIQ01 and ZIQ02) that will call the standard SAP transactions IQ01 and IQ02, respectively. Our goal is to have these custom transactions act as wrappers for the standard ones.

The reason for this approach is that our current authorization concept is not fully refined. As part of our interim solution, we intend to completely block the standard transactions (IQ01 and IQ02) via SM01, and instead allow access only through the custom transactions (ZIQ01 and ZIQ02). This would also simplify role assignments across the organization by enabling us to work solely with the Z-transactions in authorization roles.

To implement this, I used transaction SE93 and tried the following methods:

  1. Creating the custom transactions as copies of the standard ones.

  2. Using the options "Program and screen (dialog transaction)" and "Program and selection screen (report transaction)" (see attached screenshot).

However, none of these approaches worked. Each time, I encounter a system error stating that the new transaction must be maintained in table T370. I have not been able to find any official SAP Note or documentation that explains how to configure this table appropriately.

Could anyone advise on:

  • The correct procedure for creating wrapper transactions for IQ01 and IQ02?

  • Whether configuration of table T370 is mandatory for this purpose, and if so, how it should be maintained?

  • Any SAP Notes or best practices related to this kind of setup?

Thank you very much for your support and guidance.

Best regards,
Osvaldo

entry.title: Reforma tributária, CBS3 IBS3 via MIGO?
entry.content:
A Sapnote: 3569061 entrega a solução para MM, mas há NF que não são geradas por SD e são geradas via MIGO diretamente pelo movimento sem referencia a pedido.

É comum empresas terem diversos movimentos para enviar produtos a seus fornecedores via MIGO. Na J1BTAX > ADMINISTRAÇÃO DE ESTOQUES > CADEIAS DE CONTABILIZAÇÃO AI é possível ver a derivação desses casos.

Atualmente estas notas de forma Standard não vão derivar o CBS3 IB3S, teria que entrar na J1B2N e colocar manualmente ou via BADI.

Eu abri um chamado sobre o assunto na SAP perguntando se vão entregar algo para este caso, eu fiz um teste e a solução entregue não funcionou para este caso!

Eduardo

----------

Recebi a resposta abaixo, entendo então que NF que forem geradas via MOV de MM se se julgar necessário destacar CBS3 ou IB3S teremos que faze-lo via BADI.

entry.title: MI07 Problema na atribuição de impostos (J1BTAX) em NF gerada por inventário
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal!
Estou com uma situação no SAP ECC relacionada à nota fiscal gerada automaticamente por inventário, onde apenas o primeiro item da nota tem a redução de base de ICMS corretamente atribuída. Os demais itens assumem grupos fiscais genéricos (10/99), ignorando o cadastro correto da J1BTAX.

Cenário:
A nota é gerada automaticamente após movimentação de inventário (ex: MB1A / 701).

A J1BTAX está corretamente configurada para todos os materiais.

A função J_1B_IM_TX_CALCULATE_TAX_NEW é chamada para o cálculo fiscal, e percebi que o problema acontece no PERFORM get_tax_parameters, onde para os itens a partir do segundo, o sistema já não localiza os grupos corretos.

A estrutura XKOMV[] já chega preenchida na função, mas aparentemente os KSCHL não refletem as condições como acontece na MIRO.

Testes já realizados:
Executei o mesmo cenário via MIRO: os impostos são aplicados corretamente a todos os itens.

Revisei a J_1BCONDMAPV, e os KSCHL estão mapeados corretamente.

Comparei a J1BTAX e o grupo de impostos por material — está tudo certo.

Debuguei a função e percebi que após o 1º item, a lógica de atribuição de grupo fiscal não consegue recuperar os dados da J1BTAX.

Na MIRO, o grupo fiscal é atribuído corretamente a todos os itens — no inventário, apenas ao primeiro.

Pergunta:
Existe alguma particularidade na chamada da função de cálculo de imposto quando a nota é gerada por movimento de mercadoria (inventário), que afete a leitura correta da J1BTAX ou do mapeamento fiscal?

Ou ainda:

Alguma recomendação de nota SAP, configuração complementar ou workaround para garantir que todos os itens da nota de inventário tenham os impostos atribuídos corretamente?

entry.title: Discrepancy in Amounts Between SAP FM FBS and BCS Reports Post-Migration
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I have encountered an issue comparing amounts in SAP FM between FBS and BCS after executing the FM migration from FBS to BCS.

In FBS, I use the report FMEQ, while in BCS, I use GRR3. The difference lies in the Assigned column:

- In the FBS report (FMEQ), the key figure for the annual budget (Assigned) is BUF04.

- In the BCS report (GRR3), there is only one key figure available: HLST.

Regarding characteristics:

- The FBS report contains only Value Type 46 – Payment Budget: Releases.

- In BCS, I created multiple value types including 65 Funds Commitment, 66 Transfer Postings, 95 Secondary Cost Postings (CO), 50 Purchase Requisitions, 51 Purchase Orders, 60 Parked Documents, 81 Funds Reservation, 54 Invoices, 57 Payments.

And ledgers such as Funds Management Payment Budget, Funds Management Commitment Budget, Funds Management Controlling.

 

I also checked other standard FBS reports provided by SAP, such as FMRP_RFFMAV01X – Annual Budget. There, I observed discrepancies between Hard Assigned Funds and Hard Commitment/Actual values. The Hard Commitment/Actual amount matches the Assigned column in the BCS report, while the Hard Assigned Funds matches the Assigned amount in the FBS report. This amount is puzzling because when tracing document numbers related to the budget, I can't identify the source generating the total amount shown in Hard Assigned Funds. All document amounts align with the Hard Commitment/Actual value.

Additionally, I reviewed another FBS standard report, FMRP_3FMB4006 – Overview of Payment Budget, which shows a budget type KBFC – Assigned Funds. This budget type does not appear in BCS.

My questions are:

1. Does the key figure of the report column difference is the root cause? If yes, how do I define this in Report Painter? Currently, I can only select element type "key figure with characteristics" but not "predefined key figure."

2. What is the equivalent value type in BCS for Value Type 46 (Payment Budget: Releases)? I have tested adding all available value types in BCS, but the amounts don't match those in the FBS report.

3. What exactly are Hard Assigned Funds? Where does this amount originate from? How can I display it in my BCS report?

4. Is the budget type KBFC a configuration? If yes, where is it maintained? If not configuration, how can I maintain this budget type in BCS?

I appreciate any guidance or suggestions to resolve this discrepancy.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.
Regards,

Dennis

entry.title: CoA is not printing automatically after Post Goods issue for delivery.
entry.content: CoA is not printing automatically after Post Goods issue for delivery. User want to print the COA just after PGI.

entry.title: The Expected value field in PM order is not editable after creating the purchase order
entry.content: The Expected value field in Plant Maintenance order External components/services are not editable after creating the purchase order

entry.title: DELFOR D97A Mapping of Segment RFF+RE
entry.content:
Hello community,

I'm trying to complete the mapping for a DELFOR D97A to idocs for Sales Scheduling agreement update. The mapping is currently working correctly, updating the agreement. Unfortunately, for business reasons, the customer asked us to give them back in the invoice a field from the Forecast that we didn't mapped and off course we're not adding in our scheduling agreement.

The segment from DELFOR D97A is "RFF+RE" that from the customer's  guideline is is described as "Release number".

In my system I'm importing this EDI file in a DELFOR idoc type with basic type DELFOR02 and looking at the avalable segments I can't find any item field suitable for this information.

Could anyone help me understanding where this information should be written in the idoc? Is the basic type correct? As anyone experienced the same mapping issue? if yes, in which scheduling agreement's field should be written this info?

 

here below the source Delfor file I receive from the customer (sensitive data removed):

UNB+UNOA:1+XXXXXX+XXXXXX+XXXXX+101++DELFOR'

UNH+101+DELFOR:D:97A:UN'

BGM+241+20250603-3412815-0+5'

DTM+137:20250603:102'

DTM+158:20250527:102'

DTM+159:20260731:102'

NAD+SU+XXXXXXXXX.'GIS+37'

NAD+ST+XXXXXXXXXXX++6045 GG'

LIN+1++XXXXXXXX:IN'

IMD+F++:::Plate'

LOC+7+957'

RFF+ON:1045000057'

RFF+AEQ:001'

QTY+48:50:EA'

DTM+50:20250429:102'

RFF+SI:2503001346'

QTY+3:150:EA'

DTM+51:20250101:102'

DTM+52:20250602:102'SCC+1++D'

QTY+1:50'DTM+2:20250617:102'

RFF+RE:228'SCC+4++D'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20250714:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20250922:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20251027:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20251201:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20260112:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20260201:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20260301:102'

QTY+1:50'

DTM+2:20260401:102'

entry.title: VAT exchange rate
entry.content:
Hi, domestic invoice is received in foreign currency, invoice needs to have VAT value in local.  Invoice date is not necessarily a match with this VAT f/x rate used in the invoice as it can be based on delivery date.  

Is there a way to solve this in SAP ECC?  There is Note for S/4 Hana public cloud version that I think is suitable but I did not find any for ECC.

entry.title: ILM Compatibility of '/opt/doc SAP Object for Archiving
entry.content:
We are currently reviewing our document archiving strategy and need clarification on the SAP object `/opt/doc`. Specifically, we want to determine whether this object is compatible with SAP Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) for archiving and retention management, or if it only supports classical archiving. During our investigation, we noticed that `/opt/doc` is not present in the ILM Archive Repository Analysis tool (ILMARA), which raises concerns about its ILM readiness. 

Whether `/opt/doc` is an ILM-compatible archiving object or a classical archiving object.

If it is classical only, what are the best practices or recommendations for migrating to ILM or managing lifecycle policies for these documents?

entry.title: Document type field modification (BSART) in the purchase order
entry.content:
To modify the purchase order in transaction ME22N or the document type field (BSART) is being altered for purchase orders that are not in service category D.

For other purchase orders where the category field is not the service item it is being modified normally.
Please verify why purchase orders with category D Service Delivery are not altering the BSART field.

entry.title: CAPEX reporting for easy budget control
entry.content:
Hi all,

new to SAP here and it seems nothing is intuitive.

Trying to get a simple monthly CAPEX report, detailing the following for each asset:

  1. Cost Center
  2. Asset details - Cost Center, name, description, category (HW, project, machinery, etc.), Cost element if exist.
  3. Capitalization start + end date, or at least start date and capitalization period.
  4. Asset starting value, monthly depreciation amount, current book value by month
  5. Activated / not activated / expected activation date

I found some answers refering to S_ALR_87012060, others to purhcase additional SAP modules, one answer refering to SAC report (another module I can't use), and other answers which don't provide a simple solution.

Can such a simple business request, a minimum requirement by our budgeting team and function heads to better understand their cost structure, be achieved in SAP by a single transaction? or even a joining of several transactions?

Not using Hanas4.

Thank you

entry.title: VALUATION AREAS 01 AND 51 DO NOT ACCOUNT HOMOGENEOUSLY
entry.content:
When a new item is added to an already created asset account due to a purchase, the depreciation of the asset and the capital grant are consistent. In both cases, they depreciate the same amount, which is what the system should do. The problem identified arises when the addition is due to a fixed cash transfer using the ABUMN transaction. The depreciation amounts are not the same when they should be.

The conclusion is that both cases should be the same. However, they are not, and the correct calculation would be the one performed with the capital subsidy, not the accounting department.

The difference is that area 51 calculates the amortization based on the reference date and the 01 from the following month, but that happens because the asset is old (from previous years)

In this case the calculation is the same because it is based on the reference date, as it is within the fiscal year.

More information: As the system is configured, both depreciation areas must begin calculating depreciation the following month (as area 01 does). Indeed, it was determined that depreciation calculation begins the following month. However, in the case of a late asset acquisition, depreciation would also begin the following month, but taking into account the reference period indicated for the asset. Therefore, the calculation would include the months of delay from when the acquisition should have been made. For assets corresponding to unclosed fiscal years, areas 01 and 51 act uniformly, and in the opposite case, they do not, hence the discrepancy that should not occur.

entry.title: How to delete bgRFC units automatically?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to delete or Remove error units from bgRFC queue automatically without any Manual intervention.   

We know the process to delete this unit manually using SBGRFCMON however we would like to know any automated  process is available for this task.

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Sales order not approving for TAS item cat.
entry.content:
The user has created Purchase order for 1 line item without reference to the Purchase requisition no. and that too before approving the sales order. So now, the PR field in the schedule lines tab is showing blank for that line item and they are not able to approve the sales order. Also, the invoice and accounting document has been completed for this order.

Can someone please tell is there any way to approve this sales order because user is not agreeing to make a new order instead?

entry.title: I want to mass delete some old Purchase requistions but MASS not working
entry.content: I have tried using MASS to delete very old purchase requistions in SAP the MASS update says successful but it doesnt work. If i go back into MASS for the relevant PR i used for testing its unticked and also in table EBAN its not ticked. I have tried using ME57 but the closed option is greyed out. How do I get rid of all these old PR's there are hundreds of them. I can find nothing on this or any other community. Thanks Dean
